title: Heaps, Priority Queues, and Scheduling · Complexity Checkpoint
summary: Schedule incidents by urgency, arrival, and changing operational priority. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a stable priority scheduler with heap invariants. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate complete-tree shape to array indices and parent-child heap order.
Trace sift-up, sift-down, push, pop, replace, and heapify costs.
Add stable tie breakers without comparing unrelated payload objects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
